subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **162 cards** total:
  - **144** numbered cards (1-12, 12 of each number)
  - **18** Skip-Bo wild cards

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Shuffle** all 162 cards.
2. **Deal stock piles** — Each player receives 30 cards face down (20 in shorter game). This is your personal stock pile.
3. **Flip top card** of each player's stock pile face up.
4. **Place remaining cards** in the center as the draw pile.
5. **Leave space** for up to 4 building piles in the center.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Stock pile** — Your 30 (or 20) face-down cards with top card visible.
- **No hand to start** — You draw cards at the start of your turn.
- **4 discard piles** — Space for up to 4 personal discard piles (empty at start).

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Skip-Bo is a sequential card game. Be the first player to play all cards from your stock pile by building shared piles from 1 to 12 in the center.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Draw** — Draw from the draw pile until you have 5 cards in hand.
2. **Play cards** — Play cards to building piles in the center:
   - Building piles start with a 1 and go up sequentially to 12.
   - Play from your hand, your stock pile (top card), or your discard piles (top card).
   - Skip-Bo cards are wild and can be any number.
3. **End turn** — When you can't or don't want to play more, discard 1 card from your hand to one of your 4 discard piles.

#### Building Piles
- Up to 4 building piles in the center, shared by all players.
- Must start with 1 and build sequentially to 12.
- When a pile reaches 12, it's shuffled back into the draw pile.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Playing Cards
- Play from **hand** to any legal building pile.
- Play **top card of stock pile** to a building pile.
- Play **top card of any discard pile** to a building pile.
- **Priority** — Always try to play from your stock pile first.

#### Skip-Bo Wilds
- Can substitute for any number 1-12.
- Played onto building piles as whatever number is needed next.

#### Discard Piles
- You have 4 personal discard piles.
- At end of turn, you must discard 1 card from your hand to a discard pile.
- Organize strategically — you can only play the top card of each pile.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Play all 5** — If you play all 5 cards from your hand in one turn, draw 5 more and keep playing.
- **Empty draw pile** — Shuffle completed building piles to replenish.
- **Stock pile plays** — When you play your stock pile's top card, flip the next one face up.
- **No hand limit for discard** — Discard piles can grow indefinitely.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Winning
- First player to empty their entire stock pile wins.

#### Multi-Game Scoring (Optional)
- Winner scores 25 points + 5 points per card remaining in each opponent's stock pile.
- Play to 500 points.

#### Team Variant (4 or 6 players)
- Partners sit across from each other.
- You may play cards from your partner's stock pile on your turn.
- Team wins when either partner empties their stock pile.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Prioritize stock pile** — Always play from your stock pile when possible.
- **Organize discard piles** — Keep piles in order (ascending or descending) so you can access cards later.
- **Save Skip-Bo cards** — Don't waste wild cards; use them to play stock pile cards.
- **Don't help opponents** — Avoid building piles that clearly help the next player.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Discard strategy** — Put higher numbers on top so lower numbers are accessible when needed.
- **Block opponents** — Sometimes don't play a card if it would set up the next player.
- **Track the 12s** — Completing a 12 pile resets it; watch for when building piles are getting high.
- **Hand management** — Try to play all 5 cards for a free redraw.
- **Patience** — Don't rush to empty your hand if it won't clear stock pile cards.
